key_insight: The paper gives a unified convergence framework for decentralized stochastic subgradient methods with compressed communication in nonsmooth nonconvex optimization, including settings without Clarke regularity.
engineering_relevance: It enables lower-communication distributed optimization algorithms for large-scale learning and control systems where agents must coordinate under bandwidth constraints.
method: Unifies unbiased compression and contractive compression with error compensation in one decentralized framework.
Analyzes consensus-error and averaged iterates via continuous-time differential inclusions to prove global convergence.
Develops compression-based variants with sign-based regularization and gradient-tracking momentum.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports preliminary numerical experiments that support the theory and show the communication-accuracy trade-off of the proposed methods.
watch_item: The methods trade communication savings for accuracy/complexity, and the abstract indicates the empirical validation is only preliminary.
paper_title: Decentralized Stochastic Subgradient-type Methods with Communication Compression for Nonsmooth Nonconvex Optimization
